content:
What the platform does
Courts & Cases gives Indian advocates access to 1.8 million Supreme Court and High Court judgments, indexed from official court sources, covering every year from 1950 to 2026.
The advocate describes the matter — in plain language, the way they would explain it to a junior. The platform retrieves the most applicable precedents automatically. It formats the document across 40+ types: plaints, written arguments, applications, notices, revision petitions, contracts. The advocate reads the draft, checks the citations — every one of them is a real, verifiable judgment — and files.
The draft is an assistant’s first effort. The advocate’s judgment is what makes it final.
Research pulls applicable law, counter-arguments, and a strategic overview from a single description of the matter. The kind of groundwork that used to be a junior’s half-day.
Judge Intelligence builds profiles of judges from their own published judgments — how they reason, what they respond to, the patterns in how they decide. A resource that used to exist only in the memory of senior counsel is now available on the record.
Judgments provides full text of any Supreme Court or High Court order, with incoming and outgoing citations mapped, and a plain-language summary in one tap.
Vault & Team keeps the firm’s drafts, research, and client papers in one secure, searchable place — not across hard drives and email threads and physical files that take twenty minutes to locate.
Reminders tracks limitation periods and hearing dates, synced to the calendar, so nothing procedural slips.

The framing that matters
There is a generation of senior advocates who built their practice on the back of a trusted junior. Someone who knew where the cases were, who could prepare the compendium overnight, who had the plaint formatted before the morning conference.
Courts & Cases is that junior — without the leave applications.
The advocate who fights for the relief of their clients deserves, occasionally, a little relief of their own. From the files. From the citations. From the hours between receiving instructions and having something ready to take to court.
That is what this platform is for.
